inspect.getattr_static(\n            func.get_function(), \"_torchdynamo_disable\", False\n        ):\n            msg = inspect.getattr_static(\n                func.get_function(), \"_torchdynamo_disable_msg\", None\n            )\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Skip inlining `torch.compiler.disable()`d function\",\n                context=str(func.get_function()),\n                explanation=f\"Skip inlining function {func.get_function()} since it was wrapped \"\n                f\"with `torch.compiler.disable` (reason: {msg})\",\n                hints=[\n                    \"Remove the `torch.compiler.disable` call\",\n                ],\n            )\n\n        result = trace_rules.check_verbose(func, is_inlined_call=True)\n        if result.skipped:\n            from torch._dynamo.variables.misc import produce_trampoline_autograd_apply\n\n            # _origin marks this as coming from an internal dynamo known function that is safe to\n            # trace through.\n            if hasattr(getattr(func, \"fn\", None), \"_origin\") and func.fn._origin in [\n                produce_trampoline_autograd_apply,\n            ]:\n                # Known sound\n                return trace_rules.SkipResult(\n                    False, \"allowlist in dynamo known function\"\n                )\n            fn_qualname = func.fn.__qualname__ if hasattr(func, \"fn\") else \"\"\n            hints = [\n                f\"Avoid calling the function `{fn_qualname}`.\",\n            ]\n            if \"_dynamo\" not in func.get_filename():\n                hints += [\n                    f\"Apply `@torch._dynamo.dont_skip_tracing` to the function `{fn_qualname}` \"\n                    \"to force tracing into the function. \"\n                    \"More graph breaks may occur as a result of attempting to trace into the function.\",\n                    \"Please file an issue to PyTorch.\",\n                ]\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Attempted to inline function marked as skipped\",\n                context=f\"qualname: {fn_qualname}, name: {func.get_name()}, \"\n                f\"filename: `{func.get_filename()}`, skip reason: {result.reason}\",\n                explanation=f\"Dynamo developers have intentionally marked that the function `{fn_qualname}` \"\n                \"should not be traced.\",\n                hints=hints,\n            )\n\n        return result\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def build_inline_tracer(\n        parent: Any,\n        func: VariableTracker,\n        args: list[VariableTracker],\n        kwargs: Any,\n    ) -> InliningInstructionTranslator:\n        assert isinstance(\n            func,\n            (\n                UserFunctionVariable,\n                NestedUserFunctionVariable,\n                LocalGeneratorFunctionVariable,\n                LocalGeneratorObjectVariable,\n            ),\n        )\n        code: types.CodeType = func.get_code()\n        result = None\n        tracing_ctx = parent.output.tracing_context\n\n        # Check if we have already identified this function to be inline-able.\n        # The exception is dont_skip_tracing flag which affects the inline\n        # behavior. If the flag is True, don't rely on previous results.\n        if not config.dont_skip_tracing and tracing_ctx:\n            if previous_result := tracing_ctx.previously_inlined_functions.get(\n                code, None\n            ):\n                result = previous_result\n\n        if result is None:\n            if isinstance(func, SkipFunctionVariable):\n                unimplemented_v2(\n                    gb_type=\"Attempted to inline function marked as skipped (SkipFunctionVariable)\",\n                    context=f\"Attempted to inline a SkipFunctionVariable {func}\",\n                    explanation=(\n                        \"Attempted to inline a function that was previously determined to be marked as intentionally skipped.\"\n                    ),\n                    hints=[],\n                )\n            result = InliningInstructionTranslator.check_inlineable(func)\n            assert result.skipped is False\n\n            if not config.dont_skip_tracing and tracing_ctx:\n                tracing_ctx.previously_inlined_functions[code] = result\n\n        try:\n            sub_locals = func.bind_args(parent, args, kwargs)\n        except TypeError as e:\n            # Wrap the general TypeError during bind_args() to the internal ArgsMismatchError with detailed info\n            raise ArgsMismatchError(  # noqa: B904\n                \"{reason}.\\n  func = {func}, args = {args}, kwargs = {kwargs}\".format(\n                    reason=str(e),\n                    func=f\"'{func.get_name()}' {func.get_filename()}:{func.get_code().co_firstlineno}\",\n                    args=[arg.python_type() for arg in args],\n                    kwargs=kwargs,\n                ),\n            )\n\n        for v in itertools.chain(sub_locals.values()):\n            if not isinstance(v, VariableTracker):\n                unimplemented_v2(\n                    gb_type=\"Encountered unconverted argument when attempting to inline\",\n                    context=f\"func: {func}, arg: {v}\",\n                    explanation=\"An argument to an inlined function was not successfully converted to a VariableTracker.\",\n                    hints=[*graph_break_hints.DYNAMO_BUG],\n                )\n\n        if code.co_name in (\"__setitem__\", \"__setattr__\") and not (\n            args and isinstance(args[0], variables.UserDefinedObjectVariable)\n        ):\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Unsupported __setitem__/__setattr__ inline attempt\",\n                context=f\"code name: {code.co_name}, args: {args}\",\n                explanation=f\"Attempted to inline {code.co_name} where first argument (self) is not a user-defined object.\",\n                hints=[],\n            )\n\n        suffix = \"\"\n        # TODO: mlazos, add support for enabling multiple artifact logs\n        # with a single alias\n        if torch._logging._internal.log_state.is_artifact_enabled(\"bytecode\"):\n            suffix = f\"\\n{dis.Bytecode(code).dis()}\"\n        if sys.version_info >= (3, 11):\n            cur_inst = parent.current_instruction\n            parent_code = parent.f_code\n\n            def get_trace_call_log_str() -> str:\n                header = parent.get_line_of_code_header(\n                    lineno=cur_inst.positions.lineno\n                )\n                line = get_instruction_source_311(parent_code, cur_inst).rstrip()\n                return f\"TRACE inlined call {code.co_name} from {header}\\n{line}\"\n\n            trace_call_log.debug(\"%s\", LazyString(get_trace_call_log_str))\n        log.debug(\"INLINING %s%s, %s\", code, suffix, result.reason)\n\n        # Detect inline GraphModule calls in order to propagate node metadata,\n        # by checking if the first argument (self) is a variable tracking a GraphModule.\n        if args and isinstance(args[0], NNModuleVariable):\n            module = parent.output.get_submodule(args[0].module_key)\n            if isinstance(module, torch.fx.GraphModule):\n                # The inline call might not actually be a call to `forward`,\n                # but it is enough to add a context for `forward` in case it is called.\n                code_context.get_context(module.forward.__code__)[\n                    \"orig_graphmodule\"\n                ] = weakref.ref(module)\n\n        tracer: InliningInstructionTranslator\n        if is_generator(code):\n            tracer = InliningGeneratorInstructionTranslator(\n                parent,\n                code,\n                sub_locals,\n                parent.symbolic_globals,\n                parent.symbolic_torch_function_state,\n                func,\n            )\n        else:\n            # need the line below to make MyPy happy\n            assert not isinstance(func, LocalGeneratorObjectVariable)\n            tracer = InliningInstructionTranslator(\n                parent,\n                code,\n                sub_locals,\n                parent.symbolic_globals,\n                parent.symbolic_torch_function_state,\n                func,\n            )\n        return tracer\n\n    def inline_call_(self) -> VariableTracker:\n        parent = self.parent\n        code = self.f_code\n\n        strict_ctx: Any = contextlib.nullcontext()\n        if parent.strict_checks_fn:\n            strict_ctx = self.strict_translation_mode(parent.strict_checks_fn)\n        try:\n            with strict_ctx:\n                self.run()\n        except exc.ObservedException as e:\n            msg = f\"Observed exception DURING INLING {code} : {e}\"\n            log.debug(msg)\n            # bubble up the exception to the parent frame.\n            raise\n        except exc.SkipFrame as e:\n            msg = f\"SKIPPED INLINING {code}: {e}\"\n            log.debug(msg)\n            raise Unsupported(msg) from e\n        except Exception:\n            log.debug(\"FAILED INLINING %s\", code)\n            raise\n        finally:\n            parent.error_on_graph_break = self.error_on_graph_break\n\n        if self.output.should_exit:\n            # graph break\n            return ConstantVariable.create(None)  # return dummy variable\n\n        assert self.symbolic_result is not None\n\n        if self.f_globals is parent.f_globals:\n            # Merge symbolic_globals back if parent and child are in the same namespace\n            parent.symbolic_globals.update(self.symbolic_globals)\n\n        parent.inconsistent_side_effects |= self.inconsistent_side_effects\n\n        log.debug(\"DONE INLINING %s\", code)\n        self.output.tracing_context.traced_code.append(code)\n\n        if config.enable_faithful_generator_behavior or (\n            isinstance(self, InliningGeneratorInstructionTranslator)\n            and self.is_generator_from_ctx_manager\n        ):\n            if (\n                is_generator(code)\n                and isinstance(self, InliningGeneratorInstructionTranslator)\n                and self.generator_exhausted\n            ):\n                assert isinstance(self, InliningGeneratorInstructionTranslator)\n                # When the generator returns None, we raise StopIteration\n                args = []\n                if not (\n                    isinstance(self.symbolic_result, ConstantVariable)\n                    and self.symbolic_result.value is None\n                ):\n                    args = [self.symbolic_result]\n                exc.raise_observed_exception(StopIteration, self, args=args)\n            else:\n                return self.symbolic_result\n        else:\n            if is_generator(code):\n                assert isinstance(self, InliningGeneratorInstructionTranslator)\n                assert self.symbolic_result.as_python_constant() is None\n                return ListIteratorVariable(\n                    self.generated_items,\n                    mutation_type=ValueMutationNew(),\n                )\n            else:\n                return self.symbolic_result\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        parent: InstructionTranslatorBase,\n        code: types.CodeType,\n        symbolic_locals: dict[str, VariableTracker],\n        symbolic_globals: dict[str, VariableTracker],\n        symbolic_torch_function_state: SymbolicTorchFunctionState,\n        funcvar: BaseUserFunctionVariable,\n    ) -> None:\n        f_globals = funcvar.get_globals()  # type: ignore[attr-defined]\n        f_builtins = f_globals[\"__builtins__\"]\n        if not isinstance(f_builtins, dict):\n            f_builtins = f_builtins.__dict__\n\n        # Get the cached instructions. These instructions are safe to cache\n        # because we dont mutate them in transform_code_object (those\n        # instructions are for the top most Instruction translator).  Also, we\n        # have to be careful about not using _cached_cleaned_instructions here\n        # because that function is global, while we want the the cache to be\n        # alive only during a compmilation.\n        tracing_ctx = parent.output.tracing_context\n        instructions = None\n        if tracing_ctx:\n            if tracing_ctx.previously_cleaned_instructions.get(code):\n                instructions = tracing_ctx.previously_cleaned_instructions[code]\n\n        if instructions is None:\n            instructions = cleaned_instructions(code)\n            propagate_line_nums(instructions)\n            if tracing_ctx:\n                tracing_ctx.previously_cleaned_instructions[code] = instructions\n\n        super().__init__(\n            output=parent.output,\n            f_locals={},\n            f_globals=f_globals,\n            f_builtins=f_builtins,\n            symbolic_locals=symbolic_locals,\n            symbolic_globals=symbolic_globals,\n            symbolic_torch_function_state=symbolic_torch_function_state,\n            instructions=instructions,\n            code_options={k: getattr(code, k) for k in get_code_keys()},\n            f_code=code,\n            export=parent.export,\n            inline_depth=parent.inline_depth + 1,\n            speculation_log=parent.speculation_log,\n            exn_vt_stack=parent.exn_vt_stack,\n            distributed_state=parent.distributed_state,\n            package=parent.package,\n        )\n        self.funcvar = funcvar\n        self.parent = parent\n        self.num_calls = parent.num_calls\n        self.symbolic_result = None\n        self.nn_module_stack = parent.nn_module_stack.copy()\n        self.one_graph = parent.one_graph\n\n    @property\n    def fake_mode(self) -> Optional[FakeTensorMode]:\n        return self.parent.fake_mode\n\n    def run_ctx_mgr(self) -> Any:\n        return TracingContext.current_frame(self.parent.frame_summary())\n\n    def should_compile_partial_graph(self) -> bool:\n        if config.nested_graph_breaks:\n            if not self.parent.should_compile_partial_graph():\n                return False\n            return super().should_compile_partial_graph()\n        return False  # inlining functions is all-or-nothing\n\n    def create_call_resume_at(\n        self,\n        inst: Instruction,\n        all_stack_locals_metadata: Any,\n        disable_current_frame_resume: bool,\n    ) -> list[Instruction]:\n        if config.nested_graph_breaks:\n            return super().create_call_resume_at(\n                inst, all_stack_locals_metadata, disable_current_frame_resume\n            )\n        unimplemented_v2(\n            gb_type=\"Graph break in inlined function\",\n            context=\"\",\n            explanation=\"Graph breaks in an inlined call are not supported.\",\n            hints=[],\n        )\n\n    def RETURN_VALUE(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        self.symbolic_result = self.pop()  # type: ignore[assignment]\n        self.instruction_pointer = None\n        raise ReturnValueOp\n\n    def RETURN_CONST(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        self.symbolic_result = self._load_const(inst)\n        self.instruction_pointer = None\n        raise ReturnValueOp\n\n    def get_globals_source_and_value(\n        self, name: str\n    ) -> tuple[Any, VariableTracker, Source]:\n        # NamedTuple's `__new__` has a fake global scope that's not an actual\n        # module. TODO generalize the check for other non-importable cases.\n        # https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/8421b03b16a4852a527256cb7cdce2ab2d318548/Lib/collections/__init__.py#L441-L447\n        if \"__name__\" in self.f_globals and not self.f_globals[\"__name__\"].startswith(\n            \"namedtuple_\"\n        ):\n            module_name = self.f_globals[\"__name__\"]\n            module_source = self.import_source(module_name)\n            if \"torch_package\" in module_name:\n                fglobals_value = (\n                    torch.package.package_importer._package_imported_modules[\n                        module_name\n                    ]\n                )  # type: ignore[assignment]\n            else:\n                fglobals_value = _import_module(module_name)\n            # Dont use lazy vt because we will do a setattr afterwards\n            fglobals_vt = VariableBuilder(self, module_source)(fglobals_value)\n            global_source = AttrSource(module_source, name)\n        else:\n            globals_name = self.output.install_global_by_id(\n                \"___unnamed_scope\", self.f_globals\n            )\n            globals_source = GlobalSource(globals_name)\n            fglobals_value = self.f_globals  # type: ignore[assignment]\n            # Dont use lazy vt because we will do a setattr afterwards\n            fglobals_vt = VariableBuilder(self, globals_source)(fglobals_value)\n            global_source = DictGetItemSource(globals_source, name)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        if is_stdlib(fglobals_value):\n            # Users don't inplace mutate a stdlib attribute (like inspect,\n            # collections), skip guards that originate from the stdlib modules.\n            global_source = SkipGuardSource(global_source)  # type: ignore[assignment]\n\n        return fglobals_value, fglobals_vt, global_source\n\n    def _load_global(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        name = inst.argval\n        if name not in self.f_globals:\n            return self.load_builtin(inst)\n\n        if self.output.global_scope is self.f_globals:\n            # If the global scope matches that of the root frame, use handler in\n            # root frame instruction translator, to enforce consistency.\n            super()._load_global(inst)\n        else:\n            _, fglobals_vt, global_source = self.get_globals_source_and_value(name)\n            if self.output.side_effects.has_pending_mutation_of_attr(fglobals_vt, name):\n                self.push(self.output.side_effects.load_attr(fglobals_vt, name))\n            else:\n                value = self.f_globals[name]\n                self.push(VariableTracker.build(self, value, global_source))\n\n    def STORE_GLOBAL(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        if self.output.global_scope is self.f_globals:\n            # If the global scope matches that of the root frame, use handler in\n            # root frame instruction translator, to enforce consistency.\n            super().STORE_GLOBAL(inst)\n        else:\n            value = self.pop()\n            if isinstance(value, RemovableHandleVariable):\n                unimplemented_v2(\n                    gb_type=\"Storing Tensor hook handle in globals (inline call)\",\n                    context=inst.argval,\n                    explanation=\"This is not supported.\",\n                    hints=[],\n                )\n            name = inst.argval\n            _fglobals_value, fglobals_vt, _ = self.get_globals_source_and_value(name)\n            self.output.side_effects.store_attr(fglobals_vt, name, value)\n\n\nclass InliningGeneratorInstructionTranslator(InliningInstructionTranslator):\n    generated_items: list[VariableTracker]\n    # Flag whether or not the InlineGenerator should consume the entire iterator\n\n    def __init__(self, *args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> None:\n        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)\n        self.generated_items = []\n        self.generator_exhausted = False\n        self.is_generator_from_ctx_manager = False\n\n    def YIELD_VALUE(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        top = self.pop()\n        self.generated_items.append(top)\n        if len(self.generated_items) > MAX_ITERATOR_LIMIT:\n            raise exc.InfiniteGeneratorError(\n                \"Too many yield values in generator. Maybe you are inlining an infinite generator. \"\n                f\"If not, please report a bug at {PT2_ISSUE_TRACKER_URL}\",\n            )\n        self.push(ConstantVariable.create(None))\n        if (\n            config.enable_faithful_generator_behavior\n            or self.is_generator_from_ctx_manager\n        ):\n            self.symbolic_result = top\n            # Stop tracing\n            raise YieldValueOp\n\n    def GET_YIELD_FROM_ITER(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        tos = self.stack[-1]\n        if not isinstance(tos, ListIteratorVariable):\n            self.pop()\n            res = BuiltinVariable(iter).call_function(self, [tos], {})  # type: ignore[arg-type]\n            self.push(res)\n\n    def RETURN_VALUE(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        self.generator_exhausted = True\n        return super().RETURN_VALUE(inst)\n\n    def RETURN_CONST(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        self.generator_exhausted = True\n        return super().RETURN_CONST(inst)\n\n    def YIELD_FROM(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        assert len(self.stack) >= 2\n        val = self.pop()\n        tos = self.stack[-1]\n        if not (isinstance(val, ConstantVariable) and val.value is None):\n            # invoke send\n            # Unreachable code - if you hit this, you are implementing generator support and have\n            # lifted the `unimplemented(\"generator\")` in frame conversion. This codepath handles\n            # subgenerator and lines up with this line in Python 3.10\n            # https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.10/Python/ceval.c#L2599\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"Unreachable sub-generator code\",\n                context=\"\",\n                explanation=\"Should only be encountered while implementing generator support.\",\n                hints=[],\n            )\n\n        try:\n            val = tos.next_variable(self)\n        except (StopIteration, exc.ObservedUserStopIteration) as ex:\n            if isinstance(ex, exc.ObservedUserStopIteration):\n                exc.handle_observed_exception(self)\n\n            # The iterator is exhausted. Stop the loop and return.\n            self.pop()\n            self.push(ConstantVariable.create(ex.value))\n        else:\n            # Repeat the YIELD_FROM instruction in the next eval loop\n            assert (\n                isinstance(self.instruction_pointer, int)\n                and self.instruction_pointer > 0\n            )\n            self.instruction_pointer -= 1\n\n            self.push(val)\n            # Add the value to yield into generated_items and replace the top of the stack with None\n            self.YIELD_VALUE(inst)\n\n    def SEND(self, inst: Instruction) -> None:\n        assert len(self.stack) >= 2\n        val = self.pop()\n        tos = self.stack[-1]\n        if isinstance(tos, (IteratorVariable, LocalGeneratorObjectVariable)) or (\n            isinstance(tos, UserDefinedObjectVariable)\n            and isinstance(tos.value, collections.abc.Iterator)\n        ):\n            if isinstance(val, ConstantVariable) and val.value is None:\n                try:\n                    val = tos.next_variable(self)\n                except (StopIteration, exc.ObservedUserStopIteration) as ex:\n                    # To implement SEND, we have to look at the implementation\n                    # when the iterator returns StopIteration. This translates to this code\n                    # 3.11: https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.11/Python/ceval.c#L2613-L2619\n                    # 3.12: https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.12/Python/bytecodes.c#L863-L866\n                    # The implementation is different in 3.11 and 3.12. In 3.12, we rely\n                    # on END_SEND to clean up. In 3.11, SEND does the cleanup as well.\n                    if sys.version_info < (3, 12):\n                        self.pop()  # Python 3.12 uses new opcode END_SEND\n                    self.push(ConstantVariable.create(ex.value))\n                    self.jump(inst)\n                else:\n                    self.push(val)\n            else:\n                # invoke send\n                # Unreachable code - if you hit this, you are implementing generator support and have\n                # lifted the `unimplemented(\"generator\")` in frame conversion. This codepath handles\n                # subgenerator and lines up with this line in Python 3.11\n                # https://github.com/python/cpython/blob/3.11/Python/ceval.c#L2597\n                unimplemented_v2(\n                    gb_type=\"Unreachable sub-generator code\",\n                    context=\"\",\n                    explanation=\"Should only be encountered while implementing generator support.\",\n                    hints=[],\n                )\n        else:\n            unimplemented_v2(\n                gb_type=\"SEND with bad type\",\n                context=f\"TOS type: {typestr(tos)}\",\n                explanation=f\"Attempted to SEND with unsupported type {typestr(tos)}.\",\n                hints=[],\n            )\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":2479,"oldLines":7,"newStart":2479,"newLines":10,"lines":["             reason=GraphCompileReason(\"store_attr\", [self.frame_summary()]),","             stack_pops=2,","         )","-        self.output.add_output_instructions([copy.copy(inst)])","+        # copy instruction, but without exception table data","+        inst_copy = copy.copy(inst)","+        inst_copy.exn_tab_entry = None","+        self.output.add_output_instructions([inst_copy])","         self.popn(2)","         self.output.add_output_instructions(","             self.create_call_resume_at("]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

test_autograd_profiler_enabled(self):\n        def fn(x):\n            if torch.autograd._profiler_enabled():\n                return x + 1\n            else:\n                return x - 1\n\n        x = torch.randn((2, 2), requires_grad=True)\n        cnts = torch._dynamo.testing.CompileCounter()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=cnts)\n\n        if torch.autograd._profiler_enabled():\n            torch.autograd._disable_profiler()\n        assert not torch.autograd._profiler_enabled()\n        ref = fn(x)\n        res = opt_fn(x)\n        self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n\n        with torch.autograd.profiler.profile():\n            assert torch.autograd._profiler_enabled()\n            ref = fn(x)\n            res = opt_fn(x)\n            self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n\n test_autocast_cpu_graph_break(self):\n        class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n            def forward(self, x):\n                a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n                b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                d_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n\n                with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n                    e_float16 = torch.mm(a_float32, b_float32)\n                    torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                    f_float16 = torch.mm(d_float32, e_float16)\n                return f_float16\n\n        module = MyModule()\n        real = module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n        real_device = real.device\n      # Regression for: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/93890\n        def fn(x):\n            with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n                x = torch.mm(x, x)\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                x = torch.relu(x)\n            return x\n\n        x = torch.rand([4, 4])\n        self.assertEqual(x.dtype, torch.float32)\n torch.bfloat16)\n        self.assertEqual(opt_res.dtype, torch.bfloat16)\n\n    def test_autocast_cpu_graph_break_inner_fn(self):\n        class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n            @staticmethod\n            def mm_breaks(x, y):\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                return torch.mm(x, y)\n\n            def forward(self, x):\n                a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n                b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n\n                with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n                    torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                    with torch.autocast(\n                        device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16, enabled=False\n                    ):\n                        torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                        g_float32 = torch.mm(a_float32, b_float32)\n                        with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n                            # Check that nested with non-inlineable function with graph break\n                            torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                            f_float16_1 = self.mm_breaks(a_float32, b_float32)\n                    # We remember to exit the inner autocast correctly to outer\n                    # even after graph breaks\n                    f_float16 = self.mm_breaks(a_float32, b_float32)\n                    assert f_float16.dtype == f_float16_1.dtype\n                return f_float16, g_float32\n\n        module = MyModule()\n        real_16, real_32 = module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n        real_device_16 = real_16.device\n        real_dtype_16 = real_16.dtype\n        real_device_32 = real_32.device\n        real_dtype_32 = real_32.dtype\n\n        graph = torch.compile(module, backend=\"eager\")\n        out_16, out_32 = graph(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n        self.assertEqual(out_16.device, real_device_16)\n        self.assertEqual(out_16.dtype, real_dtype_16)\n        self.assertEqual(out_32.device, real_device_32)\n        self.assertEqual(out_32.dtype, real_dtype_32)\n\n        self.assertEqual(out_16.device.type, \"cpu\")\n        self.assertEqual(out_16.dtype, torch.bfloat16)\n        self.assertEqual(out_32.device.type, \"cpu\")\n        self.assertEqual(out_32.dtype, torch.float32)\n\n    def test_autocast_graph_break_method(self):\n        class MyModule(torch.nn.Module):\n            def __init__(self, bias):\n                super().__init__()\n                self.bias = bias\n\n            def mm_not_break(self, x, y):\n                return torch.mm(x, y) + self.bias\n\n            def mm_breaks(self, x, y):\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                return torch.mm(x, y) + self.bias\n\n            def forward(self, x):\n                a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n                b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\")\n\n                with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n                    with torch.autocast(\n                        device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16, enabled=False\n                    ):\n                        g_float32 = torch.mm(a_float32, b_float32)\n                    f_float16 = self.mm_breaks(a_float32, b_float32)\n\n                    assert (\n                        f_float16[0][0] == self.mm_not_break(a_float32, b_float32)[0][0]\n                    )\n                return f_float16, g_float32\n\n        module = MyModule(bias=torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16))\n\n        with torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16):\n            # Autocast doesn't work on addition, so we need the bias to be `bfloat16`\n            res = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.float32) + torch.rand(\n                (8, 8), device=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.bfloat16\n            )\n            self.assertEqual(res.dtype, torch.float32)\n\n        real_16, real_32 = module(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n        real_device_16 = real_16.device\n        real_dtype_16 = real_16.dtype\n        real_device_32 = real_32.device\n        real_dtype_32 = real_32.dtype\n\n        graph = torch.compile(module, backend=\"eager\")\n        out_16, out_32 = graph(torch.tensor([0.5]))\n        self.assertEqual(out_16.device, real_device_16)\n        self.assertEqual(out_16.dtype, real_dtype_16)\n        self.assertEqual(out_32.device, real_device_32)\n        self.assertEqual(out_32.dtype, real_dtype_32)\n\n        self.assertEqual(out_16.device.type, \"cpu\")\n        self.assertEqual(out_16.dtype, torch.bfloat16)\n        self.assertEqual(out_32.device.type, \"cpu\")\n        self.assertEqual(out_32.dtype, torch.float32)\n\n    @unittest.skipIf(not torch.cuda.is_available(), \"requires cuda\")\n    def def test_autocast_decorator(self):\n        def autocast_func(orig_func):\n            @torch.amp.autocast(device_type=\"cuda\", dtype=torch.float16)\n            def new_fwd(*args, **kwargs):\n                return orig_func(*args, **kwargs)\n\n            return new_fwd\n\n        def autocast_func_cuda(orig_func):\n            @torch.autocast(device_type=\"cuda\", dtype=torch.float16)\n            def new_fwd(*args, **kwargs):\n                return orig_func(*args, **kwargs)\n\n            return new_fwd\n\n        def autocast_func_cpu(orig_func):\n            @torch.autocast(device_type=\"cpu\", dtype=torch.float16)\n            def new_fwd(*args, **kwargs):\n                return orig_func(*args, **kwargs)\n\n            return new_fwd\n\n        def mm(a, b):\n            return torch.mm(a, b)\n\n        mm_float16 = autocast_func(mm)\n        mm_float16_cuda = autocast_func_cuda(mm)\n        mm_float16_cpu = autocast_func_cpu(mm)\n\n        def fn(a, b):\n            return mm_float16(a, b), mm_float16_cuda(a, b), mm_float16_cpu(a, b)\n\n        a_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n        b_float32 = torch.rand((8, 8), device=\"cuda\")\n\n        ref = fn(a_float32, b_float32)\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)(fn)\n        res = opt_fn(a_float32, b_float32)\n        self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n        self.assertTrue(res[0].dtype == torch.float16)\n        self.assertTrue(res[1].dtype == torch.float16)\n\n    @parametrize(\n        \"Ctx\",\n @torch._dynamo.config.patch(enable_trace_contextlib=True)\n    @parametrize(\n        \"Ctx\",\n        [CustomizedCtxManager, customized_ctx_manager],\n        name_fn=lambda x: x.__name__,\n    )\n    def test_nested_generic_context_manager_with_graph_break(self, Ctx):\n        def fn(x):\n            with Ctx(True):\n                x = x + 1\n                if torch.is_grad_enabled():\n is CustomizedCtxManager:\n                self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 4)\n                self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 4)\n\n        torch._dynamo.reset()\n        cnts =          self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n            if Ctx is CustomizedCtxManager:\n                self.assertEqual(cnts.frame_count, 4)\n                self.assertEqual(cnts.op_count, 4)\n\n    def test_graph_break_inlining_grad(self):\n        def gn(z):\n            with torch.no_grad():\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                return torch.sin(z)\n\n        def fn(x, test_context_wrapping_grad_mode_decorator(self):\n        ctx_wrappers = [(torch.enable_grad, True), (torch.no_grad, False)]\n        for call in [True, False]:\n            for i in range(2):\n                torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n                ctx_wrapper, _ = ctx_wrappers[i]\n                ctx_wrapper_inverse, mode_inverse = ctx_wrappers[(i + 1) % 2]\n\n                def fn(x):\n                    def inner_func(x):\n                        return x.sin()\n\n                    with ctx_wrapper_inverse():\n                        if call:\n                            inner_func = ctx_wrapper()(inner_func)\n                        else:\n                            inner_func = ctx_wrapper(inner_func)\n\n                        # Calling no_grad or enabled_grad should not mutate global state\n                        assert torch.is_grad_enabled() == mode_inverse\n\n                    with ctx_wrapper_inverse():\n                        return inner_func(x)\n\n                x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=True)\n                opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n                self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n                self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n\n    def test_context_wrapping_grad_mode_nested_function_decorator(self):\n        ctx_wrappers = [(torch.enable_grad, True), (torch.no_grad, False)]\n\n        for call in [True, False]:\n            for i in range(2):\n                torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n                ctx_wrapper, _ = ctx_wrappers[i]\n                ctx_wrapper_inverse, mode_inverse = ctx_wrappers[(i + 1) % 2]\n\n                def fn(x):\n                    with ctx_wrapper_inverse():\n                        if call:\n\n                            @ctx_wrapper()\n                            def inner_func(x):\n                                return x.sin()\n\n                        else:\n\n                            @ctx_wrapper\n                            def inner_func(x):\n                                return x.sin()\n\n                        # Calling no_grad or enabled_grad should not mutate global state\n                        assert torch.is_grad_enabled() == mode_inverse\n\n                    with ctx_wrapper_inverse():\n                        return inner_func(x)\n\n                x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=True)\n                opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n                self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n                self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n\n    def test_context_wrapping_set_grad_enabled_nested_function(self):\n        modes = [True, False]\n        for decorator in [True, False]:\n            for i in range(2):\n                torch._dynamo.reset()\n\n                mode = modes[i]\n                mode_inverse = modes[(i + 1) % 2]\n\n                def fn(x):\n                    with torch.set_grad_enabled(mode_inverse):\n                        if decorator:\n\n                            @torch.set_grad_enabled(mode)\n                            def inner_func(x):\n                                return x.sin()\n\n                        else:\n\n                            def inner_func(x):\n                                return x.sin()\n\n                            inner_func = torch.set_grad_enabled(mode)(inner_func)\n\n                        # Consuming set_grad_enabled by calling it on a function\n                        # should not mutate global state\n                        assert torch.is_grad_enabled() == mode_inverse\n\n                    with torch.set_grad_enabled(mode_inverse):\n                        return inner_func(x)\n\n            x = torch.zeros(10, requires_grad=True)\n            opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n            self.assertEqual(fn(x), opt_fn(x))\n            self.assertEqual(fn(x).requires_grad, opt_fn(x).requires_grad)\n\n    def test_inactive_context_graph_break_local(self):\n        def fn(x):\n            x = x + 1\n            ctx =     backends = [torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH]\n\n        @torch._dynamo.allow_in_graph\n        def check_backend_state_is_modified():\n            self.assertEqual(\n test_sdpa_kernel_ctx_manager_as_decorator(self):\n        SDPA_BACKEND_PRIORITY = [\n            torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH,\n            torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.EFFICIENT_ATTENTION,\n            torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.FLASH_ATTENTION,\n        ]\n\n        @torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(\n            backends=SDPA_BACKEND_PRIORITY, set_priority=True\n        )\n        def scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v, *args, **kwargs):\n            return torch.nn.functional.scaled_dot_product_attention(\n                q, k, v, *args, **kwargs\n            )\n\n        def f(x):\n            return scaled_dot_product_attention(x, x, x)\n\n        opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        x = torch.rand(16, 16, 64, 256, dtype=torch.float16)\n        ref = f(x)\n        res = opt_f(x)\n\n        self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n\n    # Regression test to make sure the value of set_priority is used correctly.\n    def test_sdpa_kernel_ctx_manager_set_priority(self):\n        backends = [torch.nn.attention.SDPBackend.MATH]\n        default_priority = torch._C._get_sdp_priority_order()\n\n        @torch._dynamo.allow_in_graph\n        def check_backend_priority(changed: bool):\n            self.assertEqual(\n                changed,\n                torch._C._get_sdp_priority_order() != default_priority,\n            )\n\n        def f(x):\n            with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(backends=backends, set_priority=True):\n                x = x + 1\n                check_backend_priority(changed=True)\n                x = x + 1\n\n            with torch.nn.attention.sdpa_kernel(backends=backends, set_priority=False):\n                x = x + 1\n                check_backend_priority(changed=False)\n                x = x + 1\n\n            return x\n\n        opt_f = torch.compile(f, backend=\"eager\")\n        opt_f(torch.randn(2, 2))\n\n    def test_torch_profiler_use_after_with_block(self):\n        counters.clear()\n\n        def fn(x):\n            with torch.profiler.profile() as p:\n                pass\n            p.profiler.kineto_results.experimental_event_tree()\n            return x + 1\n\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        x = torch.ones(1)\n        ref = fn(x)\n        res = opt_fn(x)\n        self.assertEqual(ref, res)\n        self.assertEqual(len(counters[\"graph_break\"]), 1)\n\n\nclass ContextlibContextManagerTests(torch._dynamo.test_case.TestCase):\n    def setUp(self):\n        self._prev = torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib\n        self._u_prev = torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest\n        torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib = True\n        torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest = True\n\n    def tearDown(self):\n        torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_contextlib = self._prev\n        torch._dynamo.config.enable_trace_unittest = self._u_prev\n\n    def test_ctx_basic0(self):\n        @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def set_default_dtype(dtype):\n            old_dtype = torch.get_default_dtype()\n            try:\n                torch.set_default_dtype(dtype)\n                yield\n            finally:\n                torch.set_default_dtype(old_dtype)\n\n        eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=True)\n        def fn():\n            with set_default_dtype(torch.float64):\n                x = torch.tensor([3.0, 3.0 + 5.0j])\n            return x\n\n        y = fn()\n        self.assertEqual(y.dtype, torch.complex128)\n        graph = eager.graphs[0]\n        actual = normalize_gm(graph.print_readable(False))\n\n        self.assertExpectedInline(\n            actual,\n            \"\"\"\\\nclass GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):\n    def forward(self):\n        set_default_dtype = torch.set_default_dtype(torch.float64);  set_default_dtype = None\n\n        x: \"c128[2]\" = torch.tensor([3.0, (3+5j)])\n\n        set_default_dtype_1 = torch.set_default_dtype(torch.float32);  set_default_dtype_1 = None\n        return (x,)\n\"\"\",\n        )\n\n    def test_ctx_basic1(self):\n        @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def compute_sin(x):\n            try:\n                yield x.sin()\n            finally:\n                pass\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def fn(x):\n            with compute_sin(x) as y:\n                return y.cos()\n\n        x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n        y = fn(x)\n        self.assertEqual(y, x.sin().cos())\n\n    def test_change_parent_nonlocal_0(self):\n        # test if a nonlocal actually gets propagated\n        z = 0\n        k = 0\n\n        def create_ctx():\n            @contextmanager\n            def ctx(x):\n                nonlocal z\n                nonlocal k\n                try:\n                    k = 100\n                    yield x.sin()\n                finally:\n                    pass\n\n            return ctx\n\n        def run_ctx(ctx, x):\n            nonlocal z\n            with ctx(x) as y:\n                z = k\n                return y.cos()\n\n @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def whoo(x):\n            y = x.tan()\n            try:\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                yield y\n            finally:\n                pass\n\n        def f(x):\n            y = x.sin()\n            with whoo(x) as z:\n                y += z.neg()\n            y += x.cos()\n            return y\n\n        x = torch.randn(2)\n        expected = f(x)\n        eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n        out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(f)(x)\n        self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n        # no graph will be @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def whoo(x):\n            y = x.tan()\n            try:\n                L.append(x.sin())\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                yield y\n            finally:\n                L.append(x.cos())\n\n        def f(x):\n            y = x.sin()\n            with whoo(x) as z:\n                y += z.neg()\n            y += x.cos()\n            return y\n\n        x = torch.randn(2)\n        eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n        y = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(f)(x)\n        self.assertEqual(y, x.sin() + x.tan().neg() + x.cos())\n        self.assertEqual(L, [x.sin(), x.cos()])\n        # no graph will be generated as we will skip all frames due to the graph break\n        self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 0)\n\n    def test_graph_break_inside_ctx_1(self):\n        @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def whoo(x):\n            y = x.tan()\n            try:\n                torch._dynamo.graph_break()\n                yield y\n            finally:\n                pass\n\n        def bar(x):\n            with whoo(x) as z:\n                return z.neg()\n\n        def f(x):\n            return x.sin() + bar(x) + x.cos()\n\n        x = torch.randn(2)\n        expected = f(x)\n        eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n        out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(f)(x)\n \"\"\"\\\nclass GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):\n    def forward(self, L_x_: \"f32[2]\"):\n        l_x_ = L_x_\n\n        sin: \"f32[2]\" = l_x_.sin();  l_x_ = None\n        return (sin,)\n\"\"\",\n        )\n        self.assertExpectedInline(\n            normalize_gm(eager.graphs[1].print_readable(False)),\n            \"\"\"\\\nclass GraphModule(torch.nn.Module):\n    def forward(self, L_stack0_: backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=False)(x)\n\n    def test_contextmanager_as_argument_only___enter__(self):\n        L = []\n\n        def h(x):\n            return x.cos()\n\n        @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def whoo(x):\n            try:\n                L.append(x.sin())\n                yield h(x) + 1\n            finally:\n                L.append(x.cos())\n\n        def fn(x, ctx):\n            y = ctx.__enter__()\n            return x + y\n\n        x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n        ctx = whoo(x)\n        eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n        y = test_contextmanager_as_argument_only___exit__(self):\n        L = []\n\n        def h(x):\n            return x.cos()\n\n        @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def whoo(x):\n            try:\n                L.append(x.sin())\n                yield h(x) + 1\n            finally:\n                L.append(x.cos())\n\n        def fn(x, ctx):\n            ctx.__exit__(None, None, None)\n            return x.sin()\n\n        x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n        ctx = whoo(x)\n        ctx.__enter__()\n        self.assertEqual(L, [x.sin()])\n\n        eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n        y = def test_advanced_contextmanager_as_argument(self):\n        def h(x):\n            return x.cos()\n\n        @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def whoo(x):\n            try:\n                yield try:\n                yield x + 1\n            finally:\n                pass\n\n        @torch._dynamo.disable\n        def g(x):\n            with whoo(x) as y:\n                return y\n\n        def fn(x):\n            return g(x)\n\n        x = torch.tensor([1.0])\n        expected = fn(x)\n\n        eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n        out = torch.compile(backend=eager, test_disable_trace_contextmanager(self):\n        @contextlib.contextmanager\n        def whoo(x):\n            try:\n                yield x.cos()\n            finally:\n                pass\n\n        def g(x):\n            return x.neg() + x.acos()\n\n        def f(x):\n            y = x.sin()\n            with whoo(x) as z:\n                y += g(z)\n            y += y.tan()\n            return y\n\n        x = torch.randn(2)\n        expected = f(x)\n        eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n        out = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False, dynamic=False)(f)(x)\n        self.assertEqual(expected, out)\n        self.assertEqual(len(eager.graphs), 2)\n\n    @parametrize(\"name\", (\"suppress\", \"stdout\", \"stderr\"))\n    def test_contextlib_suppress(self, name):\n        counters.clear()\n        eager = EagerAndRecordGraphs()\n\n        def fn(t):\n            y = t.sin()\n            # ensure we graph break on the suppress call below\n            if name == \"suppress\":\n                ctx = contextlib.suppress(ValueError)\n            elif name == \"stdout\":\n                ctx = contextlib.redirect_stdout(sys.stderr)\n            else:\n                ctx = contextlib.redirect_stderr(sys.stdout)\n\n            with ctx:\n                y += t.cos()\n            return y.tan()\n\n        t = torch.randn(2)\n        expected = fn(t)\n        got = torch.compile(backend=eager, fullgraph=False)(fn)(t)\n        self.assertEqual(expected, got)\n        self.assertEqual(len(counters[\"graph_break\"]), 1)\n        name = f\"redirect_{name}\" if def test_contextlib_nullcontext(self):\n        counters.clear()\n\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        def fn(t):\n            with contextlib.nullcontext():\n taken from modeling_reformer.py in huggingface\n    @staticmethod\n    def forward(\n        ctx,\n        hidden_states,\n        layers,\n        attention_mask,\n        head_mask,\n        num_hashes,\n        all_hidden_states,\n        all_attentions,\n        past_buckets_states,\n        use_cache,\n        orig_sequence_length,\n        output_hidden_states,\n        output_attentions,\n    ):\n        all_buckets = ()\n\n        # split duplicated tensor\n        hidden_states, attn_output = torch.chunk(hidden_states, 2, dim=-1)\n\n        for layer in layers:\n            if output_hidden_states is True:\n                all_hidden_states.append(hidden_states)\n\n            attn_output = layer(attn_output)\n            all_buckets = all_buckets + (attn_output,)\n\n        # Add last layer\n        if output_hidden_states is True:\n            all_hidden_states.append(hidden_states)\n\n        # attach params to ctx for backward\n        ctx.save_for_backward(attn_output.detach(), hidden_states.detach())\n        ctx.layers = layers\n        ctx.all_buckets = all_buckets\n        ctx.head_mask = head_mask\n        ctx.attention_mask = attention_mask\n\n        # Concatenate 2 RevNet outputs\n        return torch.cat([attn_output, hidden_states], dim=-1)\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def backward(ctx, grad_hidden_states):\n        grad_attn_output, grad_hidden_states = torch.chunk(\n            grad_hidden_states, 2, dim=-1\n        )\n\n        # free memory\n        del grad_attn_output\n\n        # num of return vars has to match num of forward() args\n        # return gradient for hidden_states arg and None for other args\n        return (\n            grad_hidden_states,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n            None,\n        )\n\n\nclass ReformerEncoder(torch.nn.Module):\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self.dropout = 0.5\n        self.layer_norm = torch.nn.LayerNorm(512, eps=1.0e-12)\n        self.layers = [torch.nn.Linear(256, 256)]\n\n    def forward(\n        self,\n        hidden_states,\n        attention_mask=None,\n        head_mask=[None] * 6,\n        num_hashes=None,\n        use_cache=False,\n        orig_sequence_length=64,\n        output_hidden_states=False,\n        output_attentions=False,\n    ):\n        # hidden_states and attention lists to be filled if wished\n        all_hidden_states = []\n        all_attentions = []\n        past_buckets_states = [((None), (None)) for i in range(len(self.layers))]\n\n        # concat same tensor for reversible ResNet\n        hidden_states = torch.cat([hidden_states, hidden_states], dim=-1)\n        hidden_states = _ReversibleFunction.apply(\n            hidden_states,\n            self.layers,\n            attention_mask,\n            head_mask,\n            num_hashes,\n            all_hidden_states,\n            all_attentions,\n            past_buckets_states,\n            use_cache,\n            orig_sequence_length,\n            output_hidden_states,\n            output_attentions,\n        )\n\n        # Apply layer norm to concatenated hidden states\n        hidden_states = self.layer_norm(hidden_states)\n\n        # Apply dropout\n        hidden_states = torch.nn.functional.dropout(\n            hidden_states, p=self.dropout, training=self.training\n        )\n\n        return ReformerEncoderOutput(\n            hidden_states=hidden_states,\n            all_hidden_states=all_hidden_states,\n            all_attentions=all_attentions,\n            past_buckets_states=past_buckets_states,\n        )\n\n\nclass ListConfig:\n    class ValueNode:\n        def __init__(self, value):\n            self.value = value\n\n        def _dereference_node(self):\n            return self\n\n        def _is_missing(self):\n            return False\n\n        def _value(self):\n            return self.value\n\n    # Based on an example from omegaconfig.listconfig\n    class ListIterator(Iterator[Any]):\n        def __init__(self, lst: Any, resolve: bool) -> None:\n            self.resolve = resolve\n            self.iterator = iter(lst.__dict__[\"_content\"])\n            self.index = 0\n\n        def __next__(self) -> Any:\n            x = next(self.iterator)\n            if self.resolve:\n                x = x._dereference_node()\n                if x._is_missing():\n                    raise AssertionError\n\n            self.index = self.index + 1\n            if isinstance(x, ListConfig.ValueNode):\n                return x._value()\n            raise AssertionError\n\n    def __iter__(self):\n        return self._iter_ex(True)\n\n    def _iter_ex(self, resolve: bool) -> Iterator[Any]:\n        try:\n            return ListConfig.ListIterator(self, resolve)\n        except Exception:\n            raise AssertionError from None\n\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        self._content = [\n            ListConfig.ValueNode(1),\n            ListConfig.ValueNode(3),\n            ListConfig.ValueNode(torch.tensor([7.0])),\n        ]\n\n\ndef longformer_chunk(hidden_states, window_overlap=256):\n    \"\"\"convert into overlapping chunks. Chunk size = 2w, overlap size = w\"\"\"\n\n    # non-overlapping chunks of size = 2w\n    hidden_states = hidden_states.view(\n        hidden_states.size(0),\n        hidden_states.size(1) // (window_overlap * 2),\n        window_overlap * 2,\n        hidden_states.size(2),\n    )\n\n    # use `as_strided` to make the chunks overlap with an overlap size = window_overlap\n    chunk_size = list(hidden_states.size())\n    chunk_size[1] = chunk_size[1] * 2 - 1\n\n    chunk_stride = list(hidden_states.stride())\n    chunk_stride[1] = chunk_stride[1] // 2\n    return hidden_states.as_strided(size=chunk_size, stride=chunk_stride)\n\n\nclass PartialT5(torch.nn.Module):\n    # Highly simplified T5Attention prefix\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self.q = torch.nn.Linear(512, 512)\n        self.k = torch.nn.Linear(512, 512)\n        self.v = torch.nn.Linear(512, 512)\n\n    def forward(\n        self,\n        hidden_states,\n        key_value_states=None,\n        past_key_value=None,\n        query_length=None,\n    ):\n        batch_size, seq_length = hidden_states.shape[:2]\n\n        real_seq_length = seq_length\n\n        if past_key_value is not None:\n            assert len(past_key_value) == 2, (\n                f\"past_key_value should have 2 past states: keys and values. Got {len(past_key_value)} past states\"\n            )\n            real_seq_length += (\n                past_key_value[0].shape[2] if query_length is None else query_length\n            )\n\n        def shape(states):\n            \"\"\"projection\"\"\"\n            return states.view(batch_size, -1, 8, 64).transpose(1, 2)\n\n        def project(hidden_states, proj_layer, key_value_states, past_key_value):\n            \"\"\"projects hidden states correctly to key/query states\"\"\"\n            if key_value_states is None:\n                # self-attn\n                # (batch_size, n_heads, seq_length, dim_per_head)\n                hidden_states = shape(proj_layer(hidden_states))\n            elif past_key_value is None:\n                # cross-attn\n                # (batch_size, n_heads, seq_length, dim_per_head)\n                hidden_states = shape(proj_layer(key_value_states))\n\n            if past_key_value is not None:\n                if key_value_states is None:\n                    # self-attn\n                    # (batch_size, n_heads, key_length, dim_per_head)\n                    hidden_states = torch.cat([past_key_value, hidden_states], dim=2)\n                else:\n                    # cross-attn\n                    hidden_states = past_key_value\n            return hidden_states\n\n        # get query states\n        query_states = shape(\n            self.q(hidden_states)\n        )  # (batch_size, n_heads, seq_length, dim_per_head)\n\n        # get key/value states\n        key_states = project(\n            hidden_states,\n            self.k,\n            key_value_states,\n            past_key_value[0] if past_key_value is not None else None,\n        )\n        value_states = project(\n            hidden_states,\n            self.v,\n            key_value_states,\n            past_key_value[1] if past_key_value is not None else None,\n        )\n\n        # compute scores\n        scores = torch.matmul(query_states, key_states.transpose(3, 2))\n\n        # (truncated here )\n        return scores, value_states\n\n\nclass ChunkReformerFeedForward(torch.nn.Module):\n    # simplified from HF modeling_reformer.py\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self.layer_norm = torch.nn.LayerNorm(256, eps=1e-12)\n        self.dense = torch.nn.Linear(256, 256)\n        self.output = torch.nn.Linear(256, 256)\n\n    def forward(self, attention_output):\n        return apply_chunking_to_forward(\n            self.forward_chunk,\n            attention_output + 1,\n        )\n\n    def forward_chunk(self, hidden_states):\n        hidden_states = self.layer_norm(hidden_states)\n        hidden_states = self.dense(hidden_states)\n        return self.output(hidden_states)\n\n\ndef apply_chunking_to_forward(forward_fn, *input_tensors):\n    # simplified from HF model_utils.py\n    assert len(input_tensors) > 0\n    tensor_shape = input_tensors[0].shape[1]\n    assert all(input_tensor.shape[1] == tensor_shape for input_tensor in input_tensors)\n    num_args_in_forward_chunk_fn = len(inspect.signature(forward_fn).parameters)\n    if num_args_in_forward_chunk_fn != len(input_tensors):\n        raise ValueError\n\n    return forward_fn(*input_tensors)\n\n\ndef _validate_model_kwargs(fn, model_kwargs):\n    # simplified from transformers.generation.utils._validate_model_kwargs\n    unused_model_args = []\n    model_args = set(inspect.signature(fn).parameters)\n    for key, value in model_kwargs.items():\n        if value is not None and key not in model_args:\n            unused_model_args.append(key)\n    if unused_model_args:\n        raise ValueError(\n            f\"The following `model_kwargs` are not used by the model: {unused_model_args} (note: typos in the\"\n            \" generate arguments will also show up in this list)\"\n        )\n\n\nclass FakeMamlInner(torch.nn.Module):\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self.linear = torch.nn.Linear(784, 5)\n\n    def forward(self, x, ignored=None, bn_training=False):\n        return self.linear(x.view(x.shape[0], -1))\n\n\nclass PartialMaml(torch.nn.Module):\n    # Highly simplified version of maml.meta.Meta.finetuning\n    def __init__(self) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self.net = FakeMamlInner()\n        self.update_step_test = 10\n        self.update_lr = 0.4\n\n    def forward(self, x_spt, y_spt, x_qry, y_qry):\n        querysz = x_qry.size(0)\n\n        corrects = [0 for _ in range(self.update_step_test + 1)]\n\n        # in order to not ruin the state of running_mean/variance and bn_weight/bias\n        # we finetuning on the copied model instead of self.net\n        net = deepcopy(self.net)\n\n        # 1. run the i-th task and compute loss for k=0\n        logits = net(x_spt)\n        loss = F.cross_entropy(logits, y_spt)\n        grad = torch.autograd.grad(loss, net.parameters())\n        fast_weights = [\n            p[1] - self.update_lr * p[0] for p in zip(grad, net.parameters())\n        ]\n\n        # this is the loss and accuracy before first update\n        with torch.no_grad():\n            # [setsz, nway]\n            logits_q = net(x_qry, net.parameters(), bn_training=True)\n            # [setsz]\n            pred_q = F.softmax(logits_q, dim=1).argmax(dim=1)\n            # scalar\n            correct = torch.eq(pred_q, y_qry).sum().item()\n            corrects[0] = corrects[0] + correct\n\n        # this is the loss and accuracy after the first update\n        with torch.no_grad():\n            # [setsz, nway]\n            logits_q = net(x_qry, fast_weights, bn_training=True)\n            # [setsz]\n            pred_q = F.softmax(logits_q, dim=1).argmax(dim=1)\n            # scalar\n            correct = torch.eq(pred_q, y_qry).sum().item()\n            corrects[1] = corrects[1] + correct\n\n        del net\n\n        accs = torch.tensor(corrects) / querysz\n\n        return accs\n\n\ndef softmax_backward_data(parent, grad_output, output, dim, self):\n    from torch import _softmax_backward_data\n\n    return _softmax_backward_data(grad_output, output, parent.dim, self.dtype)\n\n\nclass XSoftmax(torch.autograd.Function):\n    # transformers.models.deberta.modeling_deberta.XSoftmax\n    @staticmethod\n    def forward(self, input, mask, dim):\n        self.dim = dim\n        rmask = ~(mask.to(torch.bool))\n        output = input.masked_fill(rmask, torch.tensor(torch.finfo(input.dtype).min))\n        output = torch.softmax(output, self.dim)\n        output.masked_fill_(rmask, 0)\n        self.save_for_backward(output, rmask)\n        return output\n\n    @staticmethod\n    def backward(self, grad_output):\n        output, _ = self.saved_tensors\n        inputGrad = softmax_backward_data(self, grad_output, output, self.dim, output)\n        return inputGrad, None, None\n\n\nclass ModelOutput(collections.OrderedDict):\n    \"\"\"based on file_utils.py in HuggingFace\"\"\"\n\n    def __getitem__(self, k):\n        if isinstance(k, str):\n            inner_dict = dict(self.items())\n            return inner_dict[k]\n        else:\n            return self.to_tuple()[k]\n\n    def __setattr__(self, name, value):\n        if name in self.keys() and value is not None:\n            # Don't call self.__setitem__ to avoid recursion errors\n            super().__setitem__(name, value)\n        super().__setattr__(name, value)\n\n    def __setitem__(self, key, value):\n        # Will raise a KeyException if needed\n        super().__setitem__(key, value)\n        # Don't call self.__setattr__ to avoid recursion errors\n        super().__setattr__(key, value)\n\n    def to_tuple(self):\n        return tuple(self[k] for k in self.keys())\n\n\ndef create_rand_mask_from_inputs(\n    from_blocked_mask,\n    to_blocked_mask,\n    rand_attn,\n    num_attention_heads,\n    num_rand_blocks,\n    batch_size,\n    from_seq_length,\n    from_block_size,\n):\n    \"\"\"taken from HF modeling_big_bird.py\"\"\"\n    num_windows = from_seq_length // from_block_size - 2\n    rand_mask = torch.stack(\n        [p1[i1.flatten()] for p1, i1 in zip(to_blocked_mask, rand_attn)]\n    )\n    rand_mask = rand_mask.view(\n        batch_size, num_attention_heads, num_windows, num_rand_blocks * from_block_size\n    )\n    rand_mask = torch.einsum(\"blq,bhlk->bhlqk\", from_blocked_mask[:, 1:-1], rand_mask)\n    return rand_mask\n\n\nclass SequentialAppendList(torch.nn.Sequential):\n    \"\"\"from timm/models/vovnet.py\"\"\"\n\n    def forward(self, x: torch.Tensor, concat_list: list[torch.Tensor]) -> torch.Tensor:\n        for i, module in enumerate(self):\n            if i == 0:\n                concat_list.append(module(x))\n            else:\n                concat_list.append(module(concat_list[-1]))\n        x = torch.cat(concat_list, dim=1)\n        return x, concat_list\n\n\nclass momentum=momentum,\n            affine=affine,\n            track_running_stats=track_running_stats,\n        )\n        self.act = act_layer(inplace=inplace)\n\n    @torch.jit.ignore\n    def next(parameter.parameters()).dtype\n    except StopIteration:\n        # For nn.DataParallel compatibility in PyTorch 1.5\n\n        def find_tensor_attributes(module):\n            tuples = [(k, v) for k, v in module.__dict__.items() if torch.is_tensor(v)]\n            return tuples\n\n        gen = parameter._named_members(get_members_fn=find_tensor_attributes)\n        first_tuple = local_attn_chunk_length = 64\n\n\ndef _get_min_chunk_len(config):\n    \"\"\"from hf_Reformer\"\"\"\n    attn_types = config.attn_layers\n    attn_types_set = set(attn_types)\n    if len(attn_types_set) == 1 and attn_types[0] == \"lsh\":\n        return config.lsh_attn_chunk_length\n    elif len(attn_types_set) == 1 and attn_types[0] == \"local\":\n        return config.local_attn_chunk_length\n    elif len(attn_types_set) == 2 and attn_types_set == {\"lsh\", \"local\"}:\n        return min(config.lsh_attn_chunk_length, config.local_attn_chunk_length)\n    else:\n        raise NotImplementedError(\n            f\"Only attn layer types 'lsh' and 'local' exist, but `config.attn_layers`: {config.attn_layers}. Select \"\n            \"attn layer types from ['lsh', 'local'] only.\"\n        )\n\n\ndef _stable_argsort(vector, dim):\n    \"\"\"from hf_Reformer\"\"\"\n    # this function scales the vector so that torch.argsort is stable.\n    # torch.argsort is not stable on its own\n    scale_offset = torch.arange(vector.shape[dim], device=vector.device).view(1, 1, -1)\n    scale_offset = scale_offset.expand(vector.shape)\n    scaled_vector = vector.shape[dim] * vector + (scale_offset % vector.shape[dim])\n    return torch.argsort(scaled_vector, dim=dim)\n\n\ndef _get_sorted_bucket_idx_and_undo_sorted_bucket_idx(buckets):\n    \"\"\"from hf_Reformer\"\"\"\n    # no gradients are needed\n    with torch.no_grad():\n        # hash-based sort\n        sorted_bucket_idx = _stable_argsort(buckets, dim=-1)\n\n        # create simple indices to scatter to, to have undo sort\n        indices = (\n            torch.arange(sorted_bucket_idx.shape[-1], device=buckets.device)\n            .view(1, 1, -1)\n            .expand(sorted_bucket_idx.shape)\n        )\n\n        # get undo sort\n        undo_sorted_bucket_idx = sorted_bucket_idx.new(*sorted_bucket_idx.size())\n        undo_sorted_bucket_idx.scatter_(-1, sorted_bucket_idx, indices)\n\n    return sorted_bucket_idx, undo_sorted_bucket_idx\n\n\nclass CustomList1(list):\n    def __call__(self, x):\n        for processor in self:\n            x = processor(x)\n        return x\n\n    def clear(self):\n        pass  # this prevents length_times_10(self):\n        return len(self) * 10\n\n    def append_twice(self, x):\n        self.extend([x, x])\n\n\ndef _merge_criteria_processor_list(default_list, custom_list):\n    # simplified transformers/generation/utils.py\n    if len(custom_list) == 0:\n        return default_list\n    for default in default_list:\n        for custom in custom_list:\n            if type(custom) is type(default):\n                raise ValueError\n    default_list.extend(custom_list)\n    return default_list\n\n\nclass FeedForwardLayer(nn.Module):\n    def __init__(self, d_model, dim_feedforward, activation, dropout) -> None:\n        super().__init__()\n        self.linear1 = nn.Linear(d_model, dim_feedforward)\n        self.activation = activation\n        self.dropout1 = nn.Dropout(dropout)\n        self.linear2 = nn.Linear(dim_feedforward, d_model)\n        self.dropout2 = nn.Dropout(dropout)\n\n    def forward(self, x):\n        return self.dropout2(\n            self.linear2(self.dropout1(self.activation(self.linear1(x))))\n        )\n\n\nclass TransformerEncoderLayer(nn.Module):\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        d_model,\n        nhead,\n        dim_feedforward=2048,\n        dropout=0.1,\n        activation=nn.ReLU(),\n        layer_norm_eps=1e-5,\n    ):\n        super().__init__()\n        self.self_attn = nn.MultiheadAttention(d_model, nhead, dropout=dropout)\n        self.norm1 = nn.LayerNorm(d_model, eps=layer_norm_eps)\n        self.norm2 = nn.LayerNorm(d_model, eps=layer_norm_eps)\n        self.dropout = nn.Dropout(dropout)\n        self.ff_block = FeedForwardLayer(d_model, dim_feedforward, activation, dropout)\n\n    def forward(self, src, src_mask=None, src_key_padding_mask=None):\n        x = src\n        x = self.norm1(x + self._sa_block(x, src_mask, src_key_padding_mask))\n        x = self.norm2(x + self._ff_block(x))\n        return x\n\n    # self-attention block\n    def _sa_block(self, x, attn_mask, key_padding_mask):\n        x = self.self_attn(\n            x,\n            x,\n            x,\n            attn_mask=attn_mask,\n            key_padding_mask=key_padding_mask,\n            need_weights=False,\n        )[0]\n        return self.dropout(x)\n\n    # feed forward block\n    def _ff_block(self, x):\n        return self.ff_block(x)\n\n\nclass MockModule(torch.nn.Module):\n    def inner_fn(self, left, right):\n        return tuple(left) == tuple(right)\n\n    def fn(self, tensor):\n        if type(tensor) is int:\n            return False\n\n        torch.add(tensor, tensor)\n        return self.inner_fn(tensor.shape, (1, 2, 3))\n\n\nclass IncByOne:\n    def __init__(self, x):\n        self.x = x + 1\n\n\nclass IncByTwo:\n    def __init__(self, x):\n        self.x https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/113263\n    def test_unpack_hooks_dont_run_during_tracing(self):\n        def f(x, y):\n            return x * y\n\n        f_compiled = torch.compile(f, backend=\"aot_eager\")\n\n        pack_count = 0\n        unpack_count = 0\n\n        def pack_hook(x):\n            nonlocal pack_count\n            pack_count += 1\n            return x\n\n        # unpack hook shouldn't run during compilation, while we trace the forward\n        def unpack_hook(x):\n            nonlocal unpack_count\n            unpack_count += 1\n            return x\n\n        x = torch.ones(4, requires_grad=True)\n        y = torch.ones(4, requires_grad=False)\n        with torch.autograd.graph.saved_tensors_hooks(pack_hook, unpack_hook):\n            out_test = f_compiled(x, y)\n            self.assertEqual(pack_count, 1)\n            self.assertEqual(unpack_count, 0)\n            out_test.sum().backward()\n            self.assertEqual(pack_count, 1)\n            self.assertEqual(unpack_count, 1)\n\n    # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/113263\n    def test_unpack_hooks_can_be_disabled(self):\n        def f(x, y):\n            return x * y\n\n        f_compiled = torch.compile(f, backend=\"aot_eager\")\n\n        x = torch.ones(4, requires_grad=True)\n        y = torch.ones(4, requires_grad=False)\n        with torch.autograd.graph.disable_saved_tensors_hooks(\"hooks are disabled\"):\n            out_test = f_compiled(x, y)\n            out_test.sum().backward()\n\n    # https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/113263\n    def test_disabling_unpack_hooks_within_compiled_region(self):\n        def g(z):\n            with torch.autograd.graph.disable_saved_tensors_hooks(\"hooks are disabled\"):\n                return z + 5\n\n        def f(x, y):\n            z = x * y\n            return g(z)\n\n        f_compiled = out_test.sum().backward()\n\n    # See https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/issues/97745\n    def test_gan_repro_trying_to_backward_through_the_graph_a_second_time(self):\n        def f(a, b):\n            c = torch.ones(2, 2)\n            d = torch.ones(2, 2)\n            e = torch.matmul(a, c)\n            g_loss = torch.abs(e - d).mean()\n            g_loss.backward()\n            fake_d_pred = torch.matmul(b, e.detach())\n            d_loss = fake_d_pred.mean()\n            d_loss.backward()\n\n        a_ref = torch.randn(2, 2, requires_grad=True)\n        b_ref = torch.randn(2, 2, not carried over from frame to frame\n        def fn1():\n            exec(\"from torch import *\")\n\n        def fn2():\n            x = torch.zeros(4)\n            for i in range(5):\n                x = x + i\n            return x\n\n        def fn3():\n            fn1()\n            return fn2()\n\n        ref = fn3()\n        opt_fn3 = torch.compile(fn3, backend=\"eager\")\n        res = opt_fn3()\n        self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n\n    def test_with_on_graph_break_inst(self):\n        def reversible(x):\n            print(\"Hello world\")  # Cause graph break so inline fails\n            return torch.sin(torch.cos(x))\n\n        def fn(x):\n            with torch.enable_grad():\n                a = torch.sin(x)\n                b = reversible(a)\n                c = torch.sigmoid(b)\n                c.sum().backward()\n                return x.grad\n\n        x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\n        x.grad = None\n        with torch.no_grad():\n            ref = fn(x)\n\n        x.grad = None\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        with torch.no_grad():\n            res = opt_fn(x)\n        self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n\n    def test_with_on_graph_break_nested(self):\n        def reversible(x):\n            torch._dynamo.graph_break()  # Cause graph break so inline fails\n            return torch.sin(torch.cos(x))\n\n        def fn(x):\n            # nested context manager failed previously\n            with torch.no_grad():\n                with torch.enable_grad():\n                    a = torch.sin(x)\n                    b = reversible(a)\n                    c = torch.sigmoid(b)\n                    c.sum().backward()\n                    return x.grad\n\n        x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\n        x.grad = None\n        with torch.no_grad():\n            ref = fn(x)\n\n        x.grad = None\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        with torch.no_grad():\n            res = opt_fn(x)\n        self.assertTrue(same(ref, res))\n\n    # https://github.com/pytorch/torchdynamo/issues/1446\n    def test_grad_mode_carrying_correct_state_after_graph_break(self):\n        def self.foo = 0\n                return self.blah(x)\n\n            def blah(self, x):\n                return super().blah(x)\n\n        x = torch.randn(3, requires_grad=True)\n        mod = Derived()\n NumPy aggressively promotes to double, hence cast to output to correct dtype\n                    expected = get_expected(condition, x, y)\n                    result = torch.where(condition, x, y)\n                    assert torch.allclose(expected, result)\n\n                check_equal(condition, x, y)\n                check_equal(condition, y, x)\n\n        fn()\n        opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n        opt_fn()\n\n    def test_guard_fail_nested_tuple(self):\n        def fn(args):\n            return torch.ones(()), args[0] * 2\n\n        # noqa: B950\n    )\n    def test_longtensor_list(self):\n        for partition in [0, 5, 10]:\n\n            @torch._dynamo.disable\n            def rand_gen():\n                rand_vals = [random.randint(5, 10) for _ in range(10)]\n                # List of tensors mixed with np.arrays\n                return list(np.array(rand_vals[:partition])) + [\n                    torch.tensor(val) for val in rand_vals[partition:]\n                ]\n\n            def fn(x):\n                random_list = rand_gen()\n                z = torch.LongTensor(random_list)\n                return x * z\n\n            x = torch.ones(10) * 2\n\n            random.seed(0)\n            ref0 = fn(x)\n            ref1 = fn(x)\n\n            opt_fn = torch.compile(fn, backend=\"eager\")\n            # Especially for internal usage, there are many calls to random functions\n            # on first compile, e.g., from various library initializations. Run once\n            # to get that out of the way before resetting the seed:\n            opt_fn(x)\n\n            random.seed(0)\n            res0 = opt_fn(x)\n            res1 = opt_fn(x)\n\n            self.assertTrue(same(ref0, res0))\n            self.assertTrue(same(ref1, res1))\n\n    def test_primtorch(self):\n        @torch.compile(backend=\"eager\")\n        def fn(x):\n            torch._refs.abs(x)\n\n        fn(torch.randn(3))\n\n    @unittest.expectedFailure\n    #              super().__init__()\n                self.self_layoutlm_embeddings_x_position_embeddings = (\n                    torch.nn.Embedding(1024, 768)\n                )\n                self.self_layoutlm_embeddings_y_position_embeddings = (\n                    torch.nn.Embedding(1024, 768)\n                )\n\n            def forward(self, getitem_1, getitem_2, add):\n                self_layoutlm_embeddings_x_position_embeddings = (\n                    self.self_layoutlm_embeddings_x_position_embeddings(getitem_1)\n                )\n                self_layoutlm_embeddings_y_position_embeddings = (\n                    self.self_layoutlm_embeddings_y_position_embeddings(getitem_2)\n                )\n                add_1 = add + self_layoutlm_embeddings_x_position_embeddings\n                add_2 = add_1 + self_layoutlm_embeddings_y_position_embeddings\n                return (add_2,)\n\n        mod = MockModule()\n        opt_mod = torch.compile(mod, backend=\"aot_eager_decomp_partition\")\n\n        args = [\n            ((2, 512), (2048, 4), torch.int64, \"cpu\", False),\n            ((2, 512), (2048, 4),   a = 4\n            b = torch.ones(3, 4)\n\n            def __init__(self) -> None:\n                super().__init__()\n                self.c = 4\n\n            def forward(self, x):\n                return x.cos() + self.a + self.b + self.c\n\n        mod = Foo()\n        opt_mod = torch.compile(mod, backend=\"eager\", fullgraph=True)\n        args = (torch.randn(3, 4),)\n
